title: Unify calibration inventory and status matrix
outOfScope: Bootstrap Wizard and lifecycle schema changes (Work Item 7).
Ownership Manifest and update/rollback/disable/enable/uninstall implementation (Work Item 8).
Installer decomposition and complexity repayment (Work Item 10).
rawUserRequest: 开始实施 2026-07-22 Conditional GO 全面评审整改计划，严格按顺序完成当前工单及其完整 PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步；当前执行工单 6 Calibration Inventory 与 Status Matrix，不启动后续工单。用户已授权完整串行计划实施及最终发布，不得跳过任何计划内工单。

declaredIntent.summary: Unify calibration, adoption readiness, and Cockpit Status around one conservative evidence inventory with explicit provenance and stale handling.

closure.evidence: make ai-close-work-item TASK=conditional_go_calibration_scaffold_core reported ready for next Work Item on synchronized main.

sources.reason: Work Item 6 goal and acceptance requirements.
Existing readiness aggregation is the compatibility boundary that will delegate to the shared inventory.
Adoption readiness must consume the same matrix rather than recomputing independent statuses.
Cockpit Status is a required consumer of the unified inventory evidence.
Governance Status is the generated reviewer-facing status boundary.
Capability claims and evidence vocabulary must remain aligned with the inventory.
Existing readiness behavior and compatibility expectations.
Adopter fixture boundary for installed and configured evidence.
Canonical Evidence Governance and calibrated-trust terminology.

riskAssessment.reason: The matrix changes readiness and reviewer-facing status derivation; all statuses must remain fail-closed and distinguish static configuration from command or human evidence.

executionDecision.reason: The user explicitly authorized serial execution of the remediation plan; the predecessor is closed and this Contract defines the next bounded Work Item.

preReviewWarnings: Review that static file presence is never reported as command or human confirmation, and that every matrix item retains source, evidence, owner, and stale semantics.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A shared Calibration Inventory defines exactly the statuses complete, warning, incomplete, unknown, and not_applicable, and every item records source, confirmation, evidence, staleAt, owner, and blockingReason.

The inventory covers profile, guards, quality, coverage, complexity, review, security, CI, installed lifecycle, and documentation without treating defaults, static file presence, human confirmation, or command execution as interchangeable evidence.
Adoption Readiness and readiness_state consume the shared inventory while preserving existing compatibility fields and fail-closed behavior for missing or stale evidence.
Generated Cockpit Status includes a deterministic inventory summary sourced from the same matrix, and documentation/Capability Truth Matrix links the schema and status boundary.
An adopter-oriented fixture verifies complete, warning, incomplete, unknown, and not_applicable transitions, source/confirmation metadata, stale evidence, and blocking reasons.
The public Make command and installed template command surface expose inventory generation/checking without claiming that static inventory alone proves production readiness.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Calibration, Adoption Readiness, and Cockpit Status currently aggregate separate partial facts, so a reviewer cannot tell whether a result is complete, merely configured, stale, unknown, or not applicable, nor whether it came from a command, static scan, or human confirmation.
constraints: Keep AI Cockpit within the Repository Governance Layer boundary.
Preserve existing readiness_state and readiness_evidence compatibility fields while making the shared inventory authoritative.
Fail closed on missing, stale, contradictory, or unverifiable evidence; do not infer command success from file presence.
Do not implement later Bootstrap, Ownership Manifest, CI/Release Evidence, Installer decomposition, or plan cleanup Work Items.
intent.rationale: A single evidence matrix lets readiness, status, documentation, and future CI consumers use the same conservative facts and prevents the review from mistaking template/static evidence for adopter runtime proof.

evidence: tests/test_calibration_inventory.py: configured adopter fixture derives complete and warning statuses with distinct confirmations.
tests/test_calibration_inventory.py: missing, stale, and contradictory evidence remain incomplete or unknown with blocking reasons.
tests/test_calibration_inventory.py: template-only quality/lifecycle facts use not_applicable or warning without production claims.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized the complete serial remediation plan, including shared governance evidence and status changes required by Work Item 6.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
